title: cerastium latifolium var. intermedium
intro: Cerastium latifolium var. intermedium, also known as a variety of the broad-leaved mouse-ear chickweed, is a type of flowering plant in the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe and calm irritated skin and mucous membranes. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating coughs and colds, and treating digestive issues. Cerastium latifolium var. intermedium can be prepared as a herbal tea or tincture, and is sometimes used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
